note: Write-enabled finale review gate. Verified the DAPS (Decoupled Annealing Posterior Sampling; CVPR 2025; arXiv:2407.01521; github.com/zhangbingliang2019/DAPS) implementation against the canonical reference flow: (1) outer annealing over sigma high->low via the scheduler; (2) x0hat from an UNCONDITIONAL probability-flow ODE sub-sampler started at x_t with a per-level inner scheduler Scheduler(sigma_max=sigma,...) called as DiffusionSampler(...).sample(net, x_t, SDE=False), NOT a single Tweedie call; (3) clean-variable Langevin inner loop from x0hat with forward_op.gradient(x0, y, return_loss=True), denoiser outside the loop, two-term score -(x0-x0hat)/sigma^2 - data_scale*grad||A(x0)-y||^2 plus sqrt(2*lr)*z; (4) re-noise x_t = x0^(N) + sigma_next*z. Confirmed harness Scheduler properties (sigma_steps, sigma_max, num_steps) used correctly and the scaffold answer python block ast-parses. The three reused baseline traces (dps/lgd/reddiff) were left untouched. Codex stripped the 'python' fence tag from the methods context.md/reasoning.md code blocks (a misapplication of the trajectory-only no-python-fence rule); restored afterward so the methods paper-to-reasoning traces keep their ```python blocks while the trajectory reasoning files remain code-free.
